file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Cancellation Banner component that appears when a subscription is scheduled for cancellation. Contains locators for the cancellation banner UI elements including the timeline link, keep subscription button, resume modal elements, and resume confirmation banner. Used to interact with and verify the cancellation banner display and subscription resume functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Page object representing the cancellation banner component that displays when a user has scheduled their subscription for cancellation. Provides locators for all interactive elements within the cancellation banner including the link to see the cancellation timeline, the button to keep the subscription, the subscription resume modal with its confirm and back buttons, and the resume confirmation banner that appears after successfully resuming a subscription. This page object is used in test scenarios that verify cancellation banner visibility, subscription resume flows, and cancellation timeline access.

locators.ui_element_description: Link or button within the cancellation banner that opens the cancellation timeline modal showing the schedule of events after cancellation
Button within the cancellation banner that allows the user to keep their subscription and cancel the scheduled cancellation
The main cancellation banner container element that displays when a subscription is scheduled for cancellation
Resume button within the subscription resume confirmation modal that confirms the user wants to resume their subscription
Back button within the subscription resume confirmation modal that closes the modal without resuming the subscription
Confirmation banner that appears after successfully resuming a subscription, confirming the subscription is no longer scheduled for cancellation

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationBannerPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the cancellation banner is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the CancellationBannerPage object by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the base page functionality. Then defines six locator attributes on the self.elements object for all interactive elements within the cancellation banner component. Sets up see_cancellation_timeline locator using data-testid selector, keep_subscription_button using a composite selector targeting a button within the cancellation banner, cancellation_banner for the main container, resume_button and back_button for the subscription resume modal controls, and resume_banner for the confirmation message. All locators are stored as string selectors on the elements object which is inherited from BasePageObject.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a focused page object specifically for the cancellation banner component. It follows the Page Object Model pattern and extends BasePageObject. All locators are defined as string selectors on the self.elements object, which is a pattern used throughout the AURA framework. The page object contains only the constructor with no additional action or verification methods, suggesting that interaction methods are either inherited from BasePageObject or implemented in test files directly. The locators use primarily data-testid attributes which are the most reliable selector strategy. This page object is used in cancellation flow tests to verify banner display and handle subscription resume scenarios.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive page object for the subscription cancellation flow pages including the cancellation summary, cancellation feedback survey, change plan modal, pause plan options, and paper subscription cancellation. Contains extensive locators for all UI elements across multiple cancellation-related screens and modals including printer information display, cancellation confirmation buttons, retention alternatives, feedback radio buttons, timeline elements, and paper-specific cancellation options. Provides a utility method to dynamically select plan buttons based on page count.

classes.class_purpose: Comprehensive page object that encapsulates all UI elements and interactions across the entire subscription cancellation flow. Covers multiple screens including the cancellation summary page showing subscription details and printer information, the cancellation feedback survey with various reason radio buttons, the change plan modal with all available plan options, pause plan functionality, transfer subscription options, contact support links, cancellation timeline elements, and complete paper subscription cancellation flow with paper-specific feedback options. Provides locators for over 70 distinct UI elements organized by functional area including printer info, cancellation summary, retention alternatives, change plan modal, feedback survey, timeline sections, and paper cancellation elements. Contains a utility method get_plan_button() to dynamically retrieve plan selection buttons based on page count.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page containing cancellation flow screens

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the CancellationPage object by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up base page functionality. Then systematically defines 78 locator attributes on the self.elements object, organized into logical groups: printer information locators (printer_img, printer_info_name, printer_info_serial), cancellation summary elements (cancellation_summary_image, subscribed_info, confirm_cancellation_button, keep_enrollment_button), retention alternative links (change_plan_link, pause_plan_link, transfer_subscription_link, contact_hp_support_link), change plan modal elements including all plan buttons from 10 to 1500 pages, cancellation feedback survey elements with radio buttons for various cancellation reasons, timeline section locators, paper subscription cancellation elements with paper-specific feedback radio buttons, and various modal and navigation elements. All locators use a mix of data-testid attributes (most reliable), CSS class selectors, and XPath expressions for text-based matching. This comprehensive locator collection supports the entire multi-screen cancellation flow including summary, feedback, retention, and confirmation screens.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationPage object

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The page count for the plan button to retrieve (e.g., '10', '25', '50', '100', '300', '500', '700', '1500')

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the Playwright locator for a specific plan button based on the provided page count. Constructs the attribute name by formatting plan_attr as 'plan_{plan_pages}_button' (e.g., 'plan_50_button' for plan_pages='50'). Uses hasattr() to check if the self.elements object has an attribute with that name. If the attribute exists, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, plan_attr), creates a Playwright locator from that selector using self.page.locator(selector), and returns the locator object ready for interaction. If the attribute does not exist (invalid plan_pages value), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating the plan button was not found. This method provides a flexible way to select plan buttons without hardcoding the plan value in test code.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the most comprehensive page object in the batch with 78 locators covering the entire cancellation flow. It demonstrates excellent organization with logical grouping of related elements using comments. The page object uses a mix of selector strategies with preference for data-testid attributes but falls back to XPath for text-based matching and CSS classes where needed. The get_plan_button() utility method is a good example of dynamic locator selection using Python's hasattr/getattr pattern, making the page object more maintainable by avoiding code duplication for similar elements. The extensive paper cancellation feedback radio buttons show this page object supports both ink and paper subscription cancellation flows. All locators are stored as string selectors on self.elements following the AURA framework pattern.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Cancellation Timeline modal that displays the schedule of events after a subscription cancellation is confirmed. Contains locators for timeline modal elements including close button, stepper items showing cancellation stages, header elements, restore subscription button, and retention alternative links. Provides utility methods to access timeline steps and icons by index for verification of timeline progression.

classes.class_purpose: Page object representing the cancellation timeline modal that appears after subscription cancellation is confirmed. Encapsulates all UI elements within the timeline modal including the modal container, close button, timeline stepper showing cancellation stages, header icon and text, restore subscription button, retention alternative links (keep subscription, transfer subscription, shop HP), action buttons (preview bill, request recycling), and step icons for visual timeline progression. Provides five utility methods to access timeline elements by index: timeline_stepper_header() for stepper titles, cancellation_step() for step containers with wait logic, cancellation_step_title() for step titles, and third/fourth/fifth_step_icon() for accessing specific step icons. Used in tests to verify timeline display, step progression, and restoration functionality.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page containing the timeline modal

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the CancellationTimelinePage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Then defines 31 locator attributes on the self.elements object for all interactive and informational elements within the cancellation timeline modal. Sets up locators for modal controls (close_button, timeline_modal), stepper elements (stepper, cancellation_step, cancellation_step_title), header elements (header_icon, header_title, header_subtitle, timeline_img, whats_happens_next), step icons for visual progression (first_step_icon through fifth_step_icon), retention alternatives (restore_subscription, keep_subscription_as_it_was, transfer_subscription_link), action buttons (shop_hp_button, preview_upcoming_button, request_recycling_button), and navigation elements (back_to_cancel_confirmation, resume_instant_ink_subscription, see_cancellation_timeline). Many locators include multiple selector options separated by commas to handle different UI variations. All locators use primarily data-testid attributes with CSS class selectors as alternatives.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the timeline stepper header element to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific timeline stepper header element by its index position. First waits for the stepper elements to be present on the page using self.page.wait_for_selector() with the self.elements.stepper selector and a 60 second timeout. Then creates a locator for all stepper elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.stepper) and calls .nth(index) to select the element at the specified zero-based index position. Returns the Playwright locator object for that specific stepper header element, which can then be used for further interactions or assertions. The 60 second timeout ensures the timeline has fully loaded before attempting to access stepper elements.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the cancellation step element to retrieve

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds for the step elements to appear and for the specific step to be visible. Defaults to 5000ms (5 seconds)

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific cancellation timeline step element by index with built-in wait logic and error handling. First waits for the cancellation_step elements to be present using self.page.wait_for_selector() with the provided timeout. Creates a locator for all step elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.cancellation_step) and gets the total count of steps using .count(). Validates that the requested index is within the valid range by checking if index is greater than or equal to the count. If the index is out of range, raises an IndexError with a descriptive message showing the requested index and the actual number of steps found. If the index is valid, selects the step at that index using .nth(index), waits for that specific step to be visible using step.wait_for() with state='visible' and the provided timeout, then returns the Playwright locator for that step. This method ensures the step exists and is visible before returning it.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the cancellation step title element to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific cancellation step title element by its index position. Creates a locator for all cancellation step title elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.cancellation_step_title) and selects the element at the specified zero-based index using .nth(index). Returns the Playwright locator object for that specific step title, which can be used to get the title text or perform assertions. This is a simple accessor method without built-in wait logic, so the caller should ensure the timeline is loaded before calling this method.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the third step icon element to retrieve (useful when multiple third step icons exist)

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific third step icon element by index. Creates a locator for all third step icon elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.third_step_icon) and selects the element at the specified zero-based index using .nth(index). Returns the Playwright locator for that icon element. This method is useful for verifying the visual state of the third timeline step, such as checking if it displays a checkmark indicating completion.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the fourth step icon element to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific fourth step icon element by index. Creates a locator for all fourth step icon elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.fourth_step_icon) and selects the element at the specified zero-based index using .nth(index). Returns the Playwright locator for that icon element. Used to verify the visual state of the fourth timeline step.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the fifth step icon element to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific fifth step icon element by index. Creates a locator for all fifth step icon elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.fifth_step_icon) and selects the element at the specified zero-based index using .nth(index). Returns the Playwright locator for that icon element. Used to verify the visual state of the fifth and final timeline step.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object focuses on the cancellation timeline modal with 31 locators and 5 utility methods for accessing timeline elements by index. The cancellation_step() method demonstrates good defensive programming with index validation and error handling using IndexError. The timeline_stepper_header() method uses a long 60-second timeout suggesting the timeline may take time to load. The three step icon methods (third_step_icon, fourth_step_icon, fifth_step_icon) are separate methods rather than a single parameterized method, which is less DRY but may reflect different locator strategies for different steps. Many locators include multiple selector options to handle UI variations. The page object follows the AURA framework pattern of storing locators on self.elements and providing index-based accessor methods for collections of similar elements.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Change Plan modal that appears as a retention alternative during the cancellation flow. Contains locators for the change plan modal interface including plan selection buttons for different page tiers, billing cycle selection options, navigation buttons, and informational elements. Provides utility methods to select ink plans dynamically and access the back button with text filtering.

classes.class_purpose: Page object representing the Change Plan modal that appears during the cancellation flow as a retention strategy. Encapsulates all UI elements within the change plan interface including the modal container, header title and subtitle, informational icons for plan changes and shipping, plan selection buttons for all available page tiers (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700 pages), additional pages tooltip, upgrade plan section with billing cycle selection (current vs next billing cycle) including radio buttons and icons, navigation buttons (return to cancellation, back, confirm), and visual indicators like dot icons for ink plans. Provides two utility methods: select_ink_plan() to dynamically click a plan button based on page count, and back_button_choose_plan() to access the back button filtered by its text content. Used in cancellation retention tests to verify plan change options and handle plan switching during cancellation flow.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the ChangePlanCancellationPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page containing the change plan modal

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ChangePlanCancellationPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Then defines 30 locator attributes on the self.elements object for all interactive and informational elements within the change plan modal. Sets up locators for modal structure (change_plan_link, change_plan_modal, return_to_cancellation_button), header elements (change_plan_title, change_plan_subtitle), informational icons (change_plan_icon, change_plan_shipping_icon), footer message (print_less_message), tooltip elements (additional_pages_tooltip, additional_pages_tooltip_content), upgrade plan section (upgrade_plan_title, upgrade_plan_subtitle), billing cycle selection cards and radio buttons (current_bc_card, current_bc_icon, current_bc_radio, next_bc_card, next_bc_icon, next_bc_radio), navigation buttons (back_button_choose_plan, confirm_button_choose_plan), all seven plan selection buttons for different page tiers (change_plan_10_button through change_plan_700_button), and visual indicators (dot_icon_ink). All locators use primarily data-testid attributes with CSS class selectors for styled components.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the ChangePlanCancellationPage object

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The page count for the plan to select (e.g., '10', '25', '50', '100', '300', '500', '700')

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically selects an ink plan button based on the provided page count. Constructs a CSS selector string by formatting plan_selector as '[data-testid="change-plan-{plan_pages}-pages"]' where {plan_pages} is replaced with the provided value (e.g., '[data-testid="change-plan-50-pages"]' for plan_pages='50'). Creates a Playwright locator using self.page.locator(plan_selector) and immediately clicks it using .click(). This method provides a flexible way to select any available plan without hardcoding the plan value, making test code more maintainable and data-driven.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the ChangePlanCancellationPage object

functions.what_it_does: Returns a refined Playwright locator for the back button in the choose plan interface by filtering on text content. Creates a locator using self.page.locator(self.elements.back_button_choose_plan) which targets all button elements with type='button', then applies a has_text filter with the value 'Back' to narrow down to the specific back button. Returns the filtered locator object which can be used for clicking or verification. This approach is necessary because the base locator '[type="button"]' is too generic and would match multiple buttons, so the text filter ensures the correct button is selected.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object focuses on the change plan modal within the cancellation retention flow. It contains 30 locators and 2 utility methods. The select_ink_plan() method uses dynamic selector construction similar to the pattern seen in CancellationPage.get_plan_button(), but implements it differently by constructing the full selector string rather than using attribute lookup. The back_button_choose_plan() method demonstrates the use of Playwright's has_text filter to refine a generic selector, which is a useful pattern when data-testid attributes are not available. The page object includes billing cycle selection elements (current vs next billing cycle) which suggests the change plan flow includes timing options for when the plan change takes effect. All plan buttons use consistent data-testid naming convention making them easy to target dynamically.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive page object for the enrollment confirmation and plan selection flow including plan type selection, plan details review, shipping address management, billing information setup with multiple payment methods (credit card, PayPal, Google Pay), promotion code application, terms agreement, and enrollment finalization. Contains extensive locators for all UI elements across the multi-step enrollment process including pre-enrollment plan selection, OOBE (out-of-box experience) flow, FTM (first-time-member) flow, paper addon options, and various modal dialogs. Provides utility methods to dynamically select ink and paper plans based on page count.

classes.class_purpose: Massive comprehensive page object that encapsulates the entire enrollment confirmation and plan selection flow for HP Instant Ink subscription. Covers multiple enrollment paths including pre-enrollment plan type selection (monthly, yearly, pay-as-you-print), plan selection with ink-only and ink+paper options, shipping address entry and validation with suggested address handling, billing information setup supporting multiple payment methods (credit card via PGS iframe, 2Checkout, PayPal with various authentication flows, Google Pay), promotion and prepaid code application, terms and conditions agreement, automatic renewal notices, paper addon management, and final enrollment confirmation. Contains over 200 locators organized into logical sections: plan selection elements, shipping form fields and validation, billing form fields and payment method selection, PGS iframe elements for credit card entry, PayPal integration elements, Google Pay elements, promotion modal elements, confirmation page elements, OOBE and FTM specific elements, FLIP redesign elements, header navigation elements, and paper subscription elements. Provides two utility methods: select_ink_plan() and select_paper_plan() to dynamically select plans based on page count. This page object supports multiple UI variations and enrollment flows making it the central component for enrollment testing.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the ConfirmationPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page containing enrollment confirmation flow

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ConfirmationPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page). Then systematically defines over 217 locator attributes on the self.elements object, organized into comprehensive sections covering the entire enrollment flow. Defines plan selection elements including monthly_plan, ink_cartridges_tab, paper_cartridges_tab, select_plan_button, and plan type cards for pay-as-you-print, monthly, and yearly options. Sets up shipping address form fields including first_name, last_name, phone_number_input, street1_input, street2_input, city_input, state_dropdown, zip_code_input, and related buttons and validation elements. Configures billing section locators including billing_modal, credit_card_radio_option, PGS iframe elements (iframe_pgs, card_number, exp_month, exp_year, cvv_input, billing_next_button), 2Checkout iframe elements, PayPal integration elements (paypal_button, paypal_email_input, paypal_password_input, paypal_login_button, paypal_agree_button), and Google Pay elements. Defines promotion and special offer elements including promotion_code_input, promotion_apply_button, prepaid_value, and various offer type indicators. Sets up confirmation page elements including terms_agreement_checkbox, enroll_button, billing_card, shipping_card, plan_card with their respective edit buttons and content areas. Includes OOBE-specific elements, FTM redesign elements, FLIP UI elements, header navigation elements, and paper addon elements. This massive initialization ensures all possible UI elements across all enrollment flow variations are accessible for test automation.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the ConfirmationPage object

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The page count for the ink plan to select (e.g., '10', '25', '50', '100', '300', '500', '700', '1500')

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically selects an ink-only plan based on the provided page count. Constructs a CSS selector string by formatting plan_selector as 'label:has([data-testid="plan-radio-button-i_ink-{plan_pages}"])' where {plan_pages} is replaced with the provided value. This selector targets the label element that contains the radio button for the specified ink plan. Creates a Playwright locator using self.page.locator(plan_selector) and immediately clicks it using .click() to select that plan. This method enables data-driven test scenarios where the plan selection can be parameterized.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the ConfirmationPage object

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The page count for the ink+paper plan to select (e.g., '10', '25', '50', '100', '300', '500')

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically selects an ink and paper combined plan based on the provided page count. Constructs a CSS selector string by formatting plan_selector as 'label:has([data-testid="plan-radio-button-i_ink_paper-{plan_pages}"])' where {plan_pages} is replaced with the provided value. This selector targets the label element that contains the radio button for the specified ink+paper plan. Creates a Playwright locator using self.page.locator(plan_selector) and immediately clicks it using .click() to select that plan. This method parallels select_ink_plan() but targets the ink+paper plan variant.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is by far the largest and most comprehensive page object in the batch with 217 locators covering the entire enrollment confirmation flow. It demonstrates the challenge of maintaining page objects for complex multi-step flows with multiple UI variations (OOBE, FTM, FLIP redesign, standard flow). The page object imports the 're' module but does not appear to use it in any methods, suggesting it may be used in test files or was intended for future use. The two utility methods select_ink_plan() and select_paper_plan() use a different dynamic selector construction approach than seen in other page objects, using label:has() syntax to target radio button labels. The extensive locator collection includes multiple payment method integrations (PGS, 2Checkout, PayPal, Google Pay) each with their own iframe and authentication flow elements. Many locators include multiple selector options separated by commas to handle different UI versions. The organization uses comments to group related locators which aids maintainability. This page object would benefit from being split into multiple smaller page objects for different sections (ShippingPage, BillingPage, PlanSelectionPage, etc.) to improve maintainability, but the current monolithic approach ensures all enrollment elements are in one place.

key_reusable_methods: CancellationPage.get_plan_button — Dynamically retrieves plan button locator by page count
CancellationTimelinePage.timeline_stepper_header — Gets timeline stepper header by index with 60s wait
CancellationTimelinePage.cancellation_step — Gets cancellation step by index with validation and wait
CancellationTimelinePage.third_step_icon — Gets third step icon by index
CancellationTimelinePage.fourth_step_icon — Gets fourth step icon by index
CancellationTimelinePage.fifth_step_icon — Gets fifth step icon by index
ChangePlanCancellationPage.select_ink_plan — Dynamically selects ink plan by page count
ChangePlanCancellationPage.back_button_choose_plan — Gets back button with text filter
ConfirmationPage.select_ink_plan — Dynamically selects ink-only plan by page count
ConfirmationPage.select_paper_plan — Dynamically selects ink+paper plan by page count
key_reusable_locators: CancellationPage.change_plan_link — Link to open change plan modal
CancellationPage.plan_10_button through plan_1500_button — All plan selection buttons
ChangePlanCancellationPage.next_bc_radio — Next billing cycle radio button
